_comment: Position Filter — Mobile Responsive. 13 buttons in 3 rows (5/4/4) for mobile, single row for desktop. Uses responsive rules to swap chart height and format. IMPORTANT: In QuickSight, responsive rules can change chartOptions but NOT data point positions. So we pre-layout as 3 rows and use responsive rules to collapse to 1 row on desktop by setting all y=50 and adjusting x spacing. HOWEVER — QuickSight responsive rules cannot change series.data. So the practical approach is: use TWO separate visuals with conditional visibility based on a screen-size parameter, OR accept the 3-row layout as the universal mobile config.
_approach: This config is the MOBILE version. Use the original position-filter-v2.json for desktop. In QuickSight, place both visuals and use conditional visibility or free-form layout sizing to show the right one.
_setup: Parameter: PositionFilter (String, default ALL). Dataset: position-filter-data-v2.csv + calc field SelectedPosition = ${PositionFilter} + calc field RowNum = ifelse(SortOrder<=5, 1, ifelse(SortOrder<=9, 2, 3)) + calc field RowIndex = ifelse(SortOrder<=5, SortOrder-1, ifelse(SortOrder<=9, SortOrder-6, SortOrder-10)). Field Wells GROUP BY: SortOrder (col 0, sort asc), ButtonLabel (col 1), SelectedPosition (col 2), RowNum (col 3), RowIndex (col 4). Nav Action: on select, set PositionFilter = ButtonLabel.
